reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff date (April 25, 2025), there have been no credible reports of a successful coup d'état in Africa or Latin America in 2025. An attack on the presidential palace in Chad in January 2025 was reported but specifically described as foiled or failed. While the regions, particularly the Sahel, remain unstable, the frequency of successful coups appears to have declined since the 2020-2023 'contagion' period, with no successful coups confirmed in 2024 and none in the first four months of 2025. Given that only approximately two months remain (May and June 2025) and no major active coup attempts are currently reported as successful, the probability is derived from a reduced baseline risk for this short window. Typical annual rates for Africa (~2/year) would suggest a ~30% chance for a 2-month window, but the absence of successful coups for over 16 months (since Gabon in August 2023) suggests a trend of increased stability or more effective coup-prevention, leading to a lower estimate of 12%.
